item.title: Revolution Medicines / Daraxonrasib (RAS Inhibitor)
item.description:
• Summary: Revolution Medicines presented promising data on daraxonrasib, a next-generation RAS inhibitor, at AACR 2026. The drug demonstrates efficacy in pancreatic cancer and other RAS-mutant tumors, with plans for further development and potential updates at ASCO 2026. This positions Revolution as a key player in targeting oncogenic RAS pathways.
  • MoAs: Daraxonrasib is a RAS inhibitor that selectively targets mutant KRAS proteins, blocking downstream signaling pathways such as MAPK/ERK to inhibit cancer cell growth and survival.
  • Sources: STAT News, BioPharma Dive, STAT News, Oncology Pipeline, STAT News, STAT News

item.title: Innate Pharma (MATISSE Trial)
item.description:
• Summary: The MATISSE trial, presented at AACR 2026, provides a mixed clinical picture for Innate Pharma, with data on lacutamab (an anti-KIR3DL2 monoclonal antibody) in cutaneous T-cell lymphoma showing variable efficacy and safety profiles.
  • MoAs: Lacutamab: Anti-KIR3DL2 monoclonal antibody that induces antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) against malignant T-cells expressing KIR3DL2.
  • Sources: Oncology Pipeline
